title: MKOS: A Biomimetic Architecture for Knowledge Base Quality Management
description:
MKOS (Mycelial Knowledge Operating System) is an automated quality-management system for knowledge bases, using a two-phase detection pipeline inspired by biological immune systems. Detects hallucinations, staleness, bias, and contradictions in unstructured text without manual review.

Architecture: A primary fast-detection phase that flags candidate quality issues using lightweight heuristics, followed by a slower secondary verification phase that performs deep analysis only on flagged candidates. This is the biomimetic equivalent of innate (fast, broad) plus adaptive (slow, specific) immunity, applied to knowledge management.

Benchmark suite: KQAB (Knowledge Quality Assessment Benchmark) for systematic evaluation across hallucination, staleness, bias, and contradiction detection. Reproducible end-to-end. Includes a longitudinal benchmark for measuring drift over time.

Open source under Apache 2.0. Includes a paper, source code, and benchmark suite.
notes: Single-author research artifact. Two-phase biomimetic detection pipeline. Includes the KQAB benchmark suite for reproducible evaluation.
